---
metadata-files: 
  - metadata.yml
format: 
    revealjs:
      pagetitle: "Statistical MDS"
      # logo: imgs/extra/ucsd-small.jpeg
      footer: "[ISNPS • 2026]{.smaller}"
      slide-number: true
      width: 1760
      height: 990
      theme:
        - src/css/theme.scss
      include-in-header:
        - file: src/header.html
        - src/chalkboard.html
      css: src/css/imgbox.css
      filters:
        - src/scale-image.lua
      controls: true
      touch: true
      scrollable: true
      history: false
      navigation-mode: linear
      code-tools: true
      chalkboard:
        theme: whiteboard
        boardmarker-width	: 2
      # embed-resources: true
      self-contained-math: true 
jupyter: job-talk
---



## {.center}

+br+br+br

### [Multidimensional Scaling From Noisy Data]{.Large}+br [Statistical Learning & Uncertainty Quantification]{.smaller}
[Siddharth Vishwanath]{.uva-orange .Large .text-shadow}+br
+br+br

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column .center}
+br
![](imgs/extra/ucsd.png){width=50%}
+br
![](imgs/extra/uva-logo.png){width=50%}
:::

::: {.column .cemter}
![](imgs/extra/isnps.png){width=50%}
:::

::::


+br+br+br+br

::: footer
:::











# Motivation

::: {.large .center}
+br+br
Modern data is not only big, but also structurally complex. +br+br
Science, engineering, technology, and society increasingly rely+br on understanding these complex patterns in data.
:::

{{< include src/math/tex-macros >}}
$$
\require{enclose}
\require{physics}
\require{ams}
$$
















## Statistical Learning {auto-animate="false" align="center"}


::: {.footnotesize .small .center}

$\XGreen \in \bbX(\params) \hspace{2em} D = \DelXGreen + \EpsRed; \hspace{2em} \hX = \mds(D, p); \hspace{2em} \text{and} \quad \hXc(D) \in \Rnp$ be [**any estimator**]{.uva-orange} of $\XGreen$
:::


::::: {.columns .small}




:::: {.column width="51.5%" .fragment fragment-index=1 }


:::: {.r-stack}

::: {.content-box-dblue .fragment fragment-index=1 .fade-in-then-out}
**(Lower Bound).**  The reconstruction error $\ell(\hX,\XGreen)$ has:


* [**lower bound**]{} $r_n \to 0$, if:+br+br

$$
\sup_{D = \DelXGreen + \EpsRed}\pr\Big(\;\; \ell(\hXc,\,\XGreen) \gtrsim r_n \;\;\Big) \ge \frac{1}{2}
$$
:::


::: {.content-box-dblue .fragment fragment-index=2 .fade-in-then-semi-out}

**([**Minimax**]{.uva-orange} Lower Bound).**  The reconstruction error $\ell(\hX,\XGreen)$ has:

* [**minimax**]{.uva-orange} [**lower bound**]{} $r_n \to 0$, if:+br+br

$$
\inf_{\hXc} \sup_{D = \color{green}{\Del} + \EpsRed } \pr\Big(\;\; \ell(\hXc,\,\XGreen) \gtrsim r_n\;\; \Big) \ge \frac{1}{2}
$$
:::


::::

::::


:::: {.column width="0.5%"}
::::

:::: {.column width="48%" .fragment fragment-index=3 .fade-in-then-semi-out}

::: {.content-box-green}
**(CMDS Upper Bound).** The reconstruction error $\ell(\hX,\XGreen)$ has:

* [**convergence rate**]{.uva-orange} $R_n \to 0$ 
* with [**failure probability**]{.uva-orange} $\alpha_n \to 0$, if:

$$
\sup_{D = \DelXGreen + \EpsRed}\pr\Big(\;\; \ell(\hX,\XGreen) \gtrsim R_n \;\;\Big) \leq \alpha_n
$$
:::

::::


:::::


::: {.footnotesize .fragment fragment-index=4}
<p></p>

| | **Reconstruction metrics** | |
|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| **RMSE**+br+br $\displaystyle\ell\rmse(\hX,\XGreen) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{n}}\norm{g(\hX) - X}_F$ |   | **$2$-to-$\infty$ uniform error**+br+br $\displaystyle\ell\ttinft(\hX,\XGreen) = \max_{i \in [n]} \norm{g(\hx_i) - x_i}$ |
:::






## 

::: {.content-box-gray .center .footnotesize style="margin-top: -1.2%" .fragment fragment-index=1 .semi-fade-out}

| [Minimax Lower Bound]{.large .uva-orange} | &emsp;&emsp;&emsp;&emsp; | [CMDS Upper Bound]{.large .uva-orange} |
|:-------------------------------:|:---:|:-------------------------------:|
| $\displaystyle \inf_{\hXc} \sup_{D = \DelXGreen + \EpsRed } \pr\Big(\;\; \ell(\hXc,\,\XGreen) \gtrsim r_n\;\; \Big) \ge \frac{1}{2}$ | | $\displaystyle \sup_{D = \DelXGreen + \EpsRed }\pr\Big(\;\; \ell(\hX, \XGreen) \gtrsim R_n \;\;\Big) \;\le\;  \alpha_n$ |
:::

<p></p>


::: {.content-box-orange .small .fragment fragment-index=1 style="margin-top: 0%;"}

**Theorem [@vishwanath2025minimax].** &emsp; For all &emsp; $0 < r < \frac{q-4}{2}$:+br
<p></p>

| [Metric]{.uva-orange} | [Lower bound $r_n$]{.uva-orange} | &emsp;&emsp; | [Upper bound $R_n$]{.uva-orange}| &emsp;&emsp; | [ Failure Probability $\alpha_n$]{.uva-orange} |
|:---------------------:|:-------------------------------:|:---:|:-------------------------------:|:---:|:-------------------------------:|
| [$$\ell\rmse$$]{} | $\displaystyle\frac{\sigma\kappa}{\sqrt{n}}$ | | $\displaystyle\frac{\sigma\kappa}{\sqrt{n}}$ +br | | $O(n^{-2} + n^{-r})$ |
| [$\ell_{\ttinft}$]{} | $\displaystyle\underline{c}(\params)\cdot \sigma\sqrt{\frac{\log{n}}{n}}$| | $\displaystyle\bar{c}(\params)\cdot \sigma\sqrt{\frac{\log{n}}{n}}$| | $O(n^{-2} + n^{-r})$ +br+br |
<p></p>
<p></p>
:::

::: {.small .fragment fragment-index=2 .fade-down}
* $q > 4$ moments is [necessary]{.uva-orange} for consistency
* Parametric rates (in $n$) for $\ell\rmse$ and minimax-optimal up to absolute constants. Extra [$\sqrt{\log{n}}$]{.small} factor for $\ell_{\ttinft}$ is unavoidable
* Proof relies on: [(1.) orthogonal Procrustes alignment]{.magenta} + [(2.) Fuk-Nagaev-type matrix concentration]{.uva-orange}
:::

::: {.footnote .footnotesize .fragment fragment-index=1 .fade-in-then-semi-out}
where &nbsp; $\bar{c}(\params) = \kappa^2(\kappa + \Rx)$ &emsp; and &emsp; $\underline{c}(\params) = \kappa(1+\kappa\Rx)\inv$.
:::







## 
::: {#refs}
:::
